_meta.title: Choir — Dimension Enums (DB CHECK-constraint source of truth)
_meta.purpose: Every controlled vocabulary used as a DB column constraint, scoring band, or staleness rule across the Choir context data model. An engineer loads these directly into CHECK constraints, ENUM types, or a seed/lookup table. Values are VERBATIM from KB sources; do not paraphrase enum labels.

layer_legend.global_library: app-wide read-only seed data, every org references it
layer_legend.org: shared per-org record (company-context / ICP / brand DNA)
layer_legend.member: per-member record (voice_dna + prose_samples + expert_pov), org_id FK

company_size_band.detection_rule: Use employee count as primary signal; revenue as secondary when employee data unavailable. Detection signals: pricing page tier names, LinkedIn employee count, Crunchbase headcount, job posting volume, office count. (dimension-schemas.md:9, :21)

industry_level_1.usage_rule: Always record both Level-1 and a Level-2 subsector. If a company spans sectors (e.g. FinTech = Technology + Financial Services), record the primary sector the company self-identifies with, then note secondary verticals. (dimension-schemas.md:54)

raw_input_mapping.raw: UK, Europe, European, Germany, France, Nordics
Brazil, Mexico, South America, Latin America

geo_region.hq_country_format: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. US, DE, GB, JP) (dimension-schemas.md:66)

seniority_band.applies_to_column: org.icp.personas[].identity.seniority_band; member.persona_ref resolves to one of these
mapping_rules: 'Head of' -> Director band (unless company is <50 people, then VP equivalent)
'Lead' -> Manager band (unless 'Tech Lead' at enterprise = Director equivalent)
Co-founder/Founder at startup (<50) -> C-Suite. At larger company -> depends on title.

buying_committee_role.note: output-template.md §8 uses a 5-role committee map (Economic Buyer / Champion / Influencer / Technical Evaluator / Gatekeeper). persona-playbook.md:255-266 uses Economic Buyer / Champion / Influencer / Blocker / End User. Union of both supplied; engineer picks canonical set.

pain_severity.rule: Always lead outreach with P1/P2 pains. P3-P5 are follow-up angles for later sequence steps or content marketing. (dimension-schemas.md:167, persona-playbook.md:109)

pain_urgency_label.applies_to_column: org.icp.personas[].pain_hierarchy[].urgency (free-form ranked label in output-template; enumerated here for constraint)

buying_signal_category.note: Ranks 1-6 are the standard intent-buying-signals categories; 7-8 are extensions.

values.examples: Past users at new company, product alumni
Target persona moved to new company within 90d
Posting for roles your product serves
New tool adoption, stack migration, vendor switch
BOFU page visits, competitor comparison searches
New geography, new product line, M&A

values.definition: Direct buying indicator (demo request, pricing page visit, RFP issued)
Strong indirect indicator (hiring signal + funding + tech change)
Weak indirect indicator (single blog engagement, generic content download)
Product cannot function without this tool/category (hard dependency)
Better fit — faster time-to-value or stronger integration
Works with, but not a significant factor

confidence_level.applies_to_column: every sourced field (org.icp.pains[].confidence, org.identity fields, etc.) + overall_confidence rollup

values.definition: Official primary source, verified same day
values.source_examples: Official website, press release, SEC filing, named case study with URL
G2/Capterra reviews, TechCrunch/Forbes reporting, company blog, conference talk

values.definition: Single indirect source, estimated, or inferred
values.source_examples: Reddit comment, single blog mention, LinkedIn post, job posting inference

confidence_level.shorthand_alias: High|Med|Low (Med == Medium); the deep-context schema uses Med — accept both in the CHECK constraint

overall_confidence_rollup.rule: Section confidence = majority level (>50% of data points). Overall report confidence: High if 8+ sections at High/Medium; Medium if 5-7; Low if <5.

staleness_rule.applies_to_column: computed flag on any sourced field (org.identity.*, org.icp.*); drives the staleness_flags counter on the org record

rules.action: tag [STALE] and note in Data Gaps

staleness_rule.anti_fabrication_rule: If data is not found, record [NOT FOUND — searched: query1, query2]. Never estimate or fabricate. 'Not available' is always acceptable. (dimension-schemas.md:192)

business_model.note: output-template lists 'SaaS/Open-core/Marketplace/etc.' — open enum; constrain to these + 'Other' free-text.

post_status.applies_to_column: posts.status (the canonical db-schema.sql CHECK constraint) AND the RBAC draft-lifecycle state machine
post_status.source: db-schema.sql posts.status CHECK; multi-tenancy-spec.md §4.3 draft lifecycle state machine
post_status.note: Single source of truth for the post/draft lifecycle. db-schema.sql posts.status CHECK lists these EXACT values and multi-tenancy-spec §4.3 mirrors them (R-G5 / decision 8 added 'changes_requested' + 'archived' so the RBAC state machine is loadable). Keep all three in sync.

value_notes.draft: author is editing; generation gate passed
value_notes.scheduled: approved + queued for a future publish slot
value_notes.changes_requested: approver/admin asked for edits; routes back to draft

stage.source: deep-context-layer-architecture.md:181 (intake Q1.1.3); inferred funding ladder from dimension-schemas.md:13-19
stage.note: Architecture cites stage='growth' as example from intake Q1.1.3. No closed enum is fixed in the KB source; recommended canonical ladder below aligned to the company_size_band funding column.

audience_sophistication.note: Architecture cites 'Enterprise' as example value; this is a persuasion-register config knob (Schwartz awareness/sophistication lineage). No closed enum fixed in source.

values.label: High
values.definition: Industry would actively push back. Challenges a dominant practice or widely-held assumption. Content using this will generate debate.

values.label: Medium
values.definition: Some practitioners would disagree. Challenges a common but not universal practice. Content using this attracts engaged niche audiences.

values.label: Low
values.definition: Directionally accepted but under-practiced. Most would nod but few actually do it. Works for credibility-building, not differentiation.
Safe to publish in external-facing content (LinkedIn, blog, talk)
Sales-call-only — too risky for public content; raise only in 1:1 sales context

hot_take_scope.note: SKILL.md phrasing is 'Content-safe' / 'Sales-call-only'; deep-context schema canonicalizes to 'content-safe' / 'sales-only'. Use the lowercase hyphenated forms as the constraint.

tests.question: Can they name exact tools, frameworks, patterns?
Can they show this in 5 minutes?
tests.pass_criteria: Artifact, demo, or case study exists

score_action.action: Use for positioning and content pillars. Strongest differentiation.
Identify the gap. Push for the missing dimension in a follow-up question.
Internal belief only. Not ready for external-facing content. Keep in extraction doc but do not build content pillars around it yet.

tests.question: Does it connect the core beliefs into a coherent worldview?
Does it set this founder apart from competitors?
Does it define a new category or redefine how an existing category should be understood?

obi_test.rule: All three must pass for the OBI to be valid. If no single OBI emerges, present 2-3 candidates with rationale; the founder chooses.

expert_pov_extraction_mode.applies_to_column: member.expert_pov.extraction_mode (provenance of how this member's POV was captured)

values.label: Mode A: Full Interview
values.output: Complete extraction document with all 8 sections

values.label: Mode B: Async Extract
values.output: Complete extraction (may have thinner Taste Profile)

values.label: Mode C: Quick POV
values.output: Core Beliefs + Hot Takes Library + POV Clusters; OBI optional

interview_question_tag.applies_to_column: global_library.interview_questions[].tags (which question banks include this question)

values.definition: Included in Quick POV (Mode C) — 6 questions
Included in Async Extract (Mode B) — 12 questions, async-friendly variant

voice_directness.note: core-voice.md uses prose descriptors for the worked example (e.g. 'Medium-direct'). These are recommended discretized buckets for a constrainable column; store the verbatim descriptor in a companion free-text column.

voice_contrarian_tendency.note: Worked example value: 'Low-to-moderate'. Recommended discretized buckets below; keep verbatim descriptor alongside.

prose_sample_voice_mode.source: deep-context-layer-architecture.md:242 (mode example: 'Builder-Teacher'); client-voice-dna-template.md:101 ('Authoritative / Conversational / Analytical / Contrarian / etc.')
prose_sample_voice_mode.note: Open enum — client-voice-dna-template.md gives 'Voice mode: [Authoritative / Conversational / Analytical / Contrarian / etc.]'. Recommended seed values below; allow free-text.

rules: REQUIRED count: 3-5 prose samples per member.
Each sample: 200-600 words, verbatim, pre-AI preferred.
DO NOT include samples under 200 words (pattern extraction needs density). (client-voice-dna-template.md:128)
Absence is NOT a hard schema error for personal-brand content (degrades to description-only fallback, lower fidelity) BUT is a HARD STOP for brand/site copy. (deep-context-layer-architecture.md:104)

fletch_category.note: SKILL.md references 'Fletch Category 3 (POV & Insights)' and 'Cat 1-6'. The full 6-category Fletch taxonomy is NOT enumerated in these source files — only Cat 3 is named. Recorded in net_new for the data-model; engineer must source the full Fletch category list (1-6) from startup-gtm-messaging-framework before constraining.

global_library_seed_db.applies_to_column: enumerates the app-wide read-only seed DBs every org references (NOT a column constraint — a catalog of seed tables)
global_library_seed_db.note: Counts are seed-DB cardinalities cited in the architecture doc; the actual hook/framework/template/power-word CONTENT is extracted in sibling backend-build-spec artifacts (seed-data/, prompts/), not here.
